Name: Pasteis de Belem
Tips: The original 1837 nata recipe — secret and unchanged. Eat piping hot at the counter. Arrive before 9am to skip the worst queues. Dust with cinnamon and icing sugar.

Name: Manteigaria
Tips: Standing counter only. Glass-fronted oven lets you watch production. Locals' favourite rival to Pasteis de Belem. Pair with a bica espresso.

Name: Aloma
Tips: Award-winning Best Pastel de Nata 2024-2025. Crispiest shell in Lisbon. Campo de Ourique location is where locals queue.

Name: A Brasileira
Tips: Art Nouveau interior since 1905. Fernando Pessoa bronze statue outside. More atmosphere than cuisine — visit for the zinc bar and history.

Name: Fabrica Coffee Roasters
Tips: Pioneer of Lisbon specialty coffee since 2014. Visible Probat roaster. Kenyan V60 + nata from Manteigaria next door is an extraordinary pairing.

Name: Copenhagen Coffee Lab
Address: Multiple locations: Rua de Sao Paulo 60-62 (Cais do Sodre) and Rua Nova da Piedade 10 (Principe Real)
Tips: Best flat white in Lisbon. Danish cinnamon buns are exceptional. Six locations across city. The Cais do Sodre branch is near Time Out Market.

Name: Bettina e Niccolo Corallo
Tips: Family plantation on Sao Tome e Principe (West Africa). Drinking chocolate and chocolate sorbet made from their own cacao. Single-origin espresso. A unique provenance story.

Name: A Padaria Portuguesa
Address: Multiple — especially Largo da Graca 14 and Principe Real
Tips: 70+ locations citywide. Pao de Deus (coconut brioche) and pistachio croissants are standouts. Meal deal soup + sandwich + juice from 5 EUR. Largo da Graca branch is perfect before the Graca viewpoint.

Name: Pastelaria Alfama Doce
Tips: Hidden gem in Alfama alleyways. Traditional glass counter of pastries. Multilingual owner. One of the best natas in the neighbourhood. Perfect before starting an Alfama walking tour.

Name: Cafe O Corvo
Tips: TripAdvisor 4.6/5. Vintage cinema chairs stone countertops. Open 9am-midnight daily. Perfect before or after visiting Castelo or Igreja de Sao Domingos.

Name: Dear Breakfast
Address: Multiple: Chiado Principe Real Santos Baixa
Tips: Consistent quality across locations. Relaxed daytime atmosphere. Popular with locals and expats. Chiado location near Tram 28 route.

Name: Nannarella Gelati alla Romana
Address: Rua Nova da Piedade 64A 1200-299 Lisboa (Sao Bento)
Tips: Featured on Netflix Somebody Feed Phil. Best gelato in Lisbon by most local accounts. Italian alla romana style. Walk 2 min to Praca das Flores garden to sit. Go at opening or late evening to avoid summer lines.

Name: Artisani
Address: Av Alvares Cabral 65B (main) and Doca de Santo Amaro Alcantara (near bridge)
Tips: 100% Portuguese ingredients. 50 flavours including dairy-free sorbets. Pastel de nata flavour gelato is extraordinary. Doca de Santo branch has waterfront views near Ponte 25 de Abril — perfect after Belem.

Name: Gelados Santini
Address: Rua do Carmo 9 1200-093 Lisboa (Chiado) and Cais do Sodre
Tips: Founded 1949 in Estoril. Only fresh fruit no artificial anything. Locals call this the best glacier in Lisbon. Pistachio and lemon are the classics. Torrone (almond caramel) is exceptional.

Name: Gelato Davvero
Address: Multiple: Rua de Sao Cristovao 14 (Alfama edge) and Av Dom Carlos I 39 (Santos) and Rua Cais de Santarem 36 (riverside)
Tips: Authentic Italian family-run gelateria since 2015. Rated 4.9/5. The Alfama-edge branch at Rua de Sao Cristovao fits a Castelo to Se to ice cream route perfectly.

Name: Time Out Market Lisboa
Address: Av 24 de Julho 49 1200-479 Lisboa (Cais do Sodre)
Tips: 26 curated restaurants under one roof. Genuinely high quality — not a tourist trap. Everyone picks something different. Octopus grilled fish bifana nata and vinho verde are the must-orders. Best at lunch on a weekday 11am-1pm.

Name: Mercado de Campo de Ourique
Tips: Much calmer and more local than Time Out. Fresh produce butchers cheese vendors and food hall. Perfect Sunday morning while Aloma is steps away.

Name: Cervejaria Ramiro
Tips: Lisbon legend since 1956. No reservations — arrive at 19:00 opening. Order: garlic prawns razor clams percebes. Finish with a prego steak sandwich — the local ritual. Queue is worth it.

Name: Solar dos Presuntos
Address: Rua das Portas de Santo Antao 150 1150-269 Lisboa
Tips: Lisbon institution since 1974. Azulejo-lined dining room serious wine list. Best acorda de marisco (shellfish bread stew) in the city.

Name: Laurentina O Rei do Bacalhau
Tips: Most extensive bacalhau menu in Lisbon — 12+ preparations. 9.4/10 TheFork (14900+ reviews). Also serves Mozambican-influenced dishes. Closed Sundays. Mon-Sat 12pm-4pm and 7pm-11pm.

Name: A Cevicheria
Address: Rua Dom Pedro V 129 1250-097 Lisboa (Principe Real)
Tips: Chef Kiko Martins applies Portuguese-Atlantic ingredients to Peruvian technique. The rubber octopus suspended from the ceiling is an Instagrammed landmark. Signature ceviche and octopus tiradito.

Name: O Velho Eurico
Address: Largo Sao Cristovao 3 (also listed as Beco do Carneiro 10) Alfama
Tips: Cult tasca — book weeks ahead. Grilled sardines walls covered in fado photos open kitchen tables in the street. Bacalhau a Bras and pork belly petiscos are legendary. Loud music welcoming atmosphere.

Name: Ze da Mouraria
Tips: Most authentic cheap Portuguese lunch in central Lisbon. Over 200kg of bacalhau a minhota sold weekly. Spontaneous fado performances possible. Arrive at noon or 1:30pm.

Name: Pateo 13
Tips: August = sardine peak season. Outdoor-only terrace in a charming Alfama square. Charcoal grill. Sardinhas assadas with boiled potatoes. Verify open on arrival.

Name: Taberna do Mar
Tips: Taste of Lisboa recommended. Only 2 meat dishes — everything from the sea. Sardine nigiri smoked red mullet sashimi cockles in Bulhao Pato sauce. Steps from Graca viewpoint.

Name: Taberna Moderna
Tips: Chef-driven sharing concept. Cuttlefish rice braised tuna ham and cuttlefish croquettes ovos rotos with squid and shrimp. Outdoor terrace.

Name: Os Gazeteiros
Tips: Best value splurge in Alfama. Set menu: 4 small entrees + 2 desserts ~35 EUR. Reviewers call it Noma in Lisbon. Dinner only Tue-Sat. Book ahead.

Name: Tasca da Esquina
Address: Rua Domingos Sequeira 41C 1350-119 Lisboa (Campo de Ourique)
Tips: Chef Vitor Sobral. Traditional petiscos elevated with modern technique. Scallops with passion fruit emulsion tuna tartare octopus with mango. Local neighbourhood away from tourist crowds. 9.4/10 TheFork.

Name: Taberna da Rua das Flores
Tips: Chef writes menu daily on a chalkboard — no printed menu no reservations. Best petiscos in Lisbon per TripAdvisor. Amejoas a Bulhao Pato fresh oysters seasonal specials. Arrive at noon for best chance at a table.

Name: Restaurante Pica-Pau
Address: Rua da Escola Politecnica 27 1200-244 Lisboa (Principe Real)
Tips: Named for the pica-pau petisco — garlic beef cubes on toothpicks. Leafy patio excellent for families. Also lagareiro octopus feijoada codfish fritters charcuterie. Eating Europe top pick for pica-pau.

Name: Oficio Tasco Atipico
Tips: Atypical tasca — light creative petiscos with international touches. Mackerel with lettuce vinaigrette beef tartare with bone marrow. The bolo de bolacha dessert (Marie biscuits coffee buttercream chocolate ganache) is legendary.

Name: Tasca Baldracca
Tips: Part of the chef collective reshaping Lisbons tasca scene near O Velho Eurico. Rotating sharing plates great vibes. A place you will easily find yourself sitting at for hours. Good backup if O Velho Eurico is full.

Name: As Bifanas do Afonso
Address: Rua da Madalena 146 Baixa (near Se Cathedral)
Tips: The best bifana in Lisbon — Anthony Bourdain ate here. Pork in garlic wine sauce in a crusty roll. A masterclass in simplicity. Daytime hours cash preferred.

Name: Casa das Bifanas
Tips: Classic Baixa bifana option. Steps from Rossio Square. Soft juicy perfectly seasoned sauce leans tangy and garlicky.

Name: Casa Portuguesa do Pastel de Bacalhau
Tips: Classic codfish croquettes with Serra da Estrela cheese filling. Old-school interior on Lisbons main pedestrian street. Daily 10am-11pm. Great mid-sightseeing snack.

Name: Loja das Conservas
Address: Rua do Arsenal 130 1100-038 Lisboa (near Praca do Comercio)
Tips: 300+ different canned fish. Beautiful white-tiled interior. Run by Portuguese National Association of Canned Fish Manufacturers. Premium brands: Pinhais Nuri Ramirez. Staff give tastings. Best souvenir in Lisbon. Mon-Sat 10am-8pm Sun 12pm-8pm.

Name: Conserveira de Lisboa
Address: Rua dos Bacalhoeiros 34 Baixa (near Alfama edge)
Tips: 84+ years of history. Family-owned production. Tastings offered. Kind helpful staff. Their own gourmet brand.

Name: A Ginjinha
Tips: Original ginjinha bar since 1840. Standing room only. Order com elas (with cherries) or sem elas (without). The adjacent Igreja de Sao Domingos is atmospheric and worth stepping inside. A 5-minute ritual that costs pocket change.

Name: Ginjinha Sem Rival
Address: Rua das Portas de Santo Antao (Rossio area)
Tips: Family-owned since 1890. Spotted by Locals recommended. Same standing-room tradition as A Ginjinha. Slightly different recipe.

Name: Tico Tico
Tips: August visit falls in peak caracol season (May-Aug). Eating Europe top pick for caracois. Garlic and oregano broth with bread to soak it up. Adventurous eating experience for kids. Daily 12pm-10pm.

Name: Taberna Sal Grosso
Tips: Taste of Lisboa top pick. Rotating market-driven chalkboard menu. Bread with garlic-infused lard. Legendary chocolate mousse with fleur de sel and olive oil drizzle. Near Santa Apolonia station.

Name: Pigmeu
Address: Rua 4 de Infantaria 68 1350-274 Lisboa (Campo de Ourique)
Tips: Every dish contains pork. Nose-to-tail Alentejano pork philosophy. Croquetes pasteis de massa tenra suckling pig confit. Authentic neighbourhood away from tourist crowds. Taste of Lisboa recommended.

Name: Taberna Albricoque
Address: Rua Caminhos de Ferro 98 1100-395 Lisboa (Alfama edge)
Tips: Algarve-focused contemporary tavern — unique in Lisbon. Moorish-influenced southern cuisine. Ray fish with garlic sauce chicken with figs and almonds rissol filled with cockles.

Name: Doca de Santo
Address: Av de Brasil Doca de Santo Amaro 1350-352 Lisboa
Tips: Full kids menu high chairs small play area — genuinely rare in Lisbon. Waterfront terrace facing 25 de Abril bridge. Relaxed family dinner.

Name: Sto Restaurante
Tips: Warm neighbourhood-feel seasonal Portuguese menu. Genuinely local crowd. Good mid-range in a neighbourhood full of overpriced tourist traps.

Name: A Tasca do Chico
Address: Rua do Diario de Noticias 39 1200-141 Lisboa (Bairro Alto)
Tips: Most authentic fado vadio in Lisbon. Fado Mon and Wed evenings only — raw unscripted unforgettable. Arrive by 20:30. Cash only. Tiny space book ahead.

Name: Sr Fado
Tips: Mid-size Alfama fado restaurant. More polished than Tasca do Chico but still authentic. Multiple performers good food. Better for families wanting the experience in less cramped setting. Book ahead.

Name: Restaurante Clube de Fado
Address: Rua de Sao Joao da Praca 92 1100-521 Lisboa (Alfama)
Tips: Most prestigious fado dinner option. Founded by celebrated fado guitarist excellent performers superior food and wine. Book well in advance.

Name: Park Bar
Tips: Best sunset view in Lisbon — 25 de Abril bridge Tagus and city spread below. Hidden on top floor of a car park. Arrive before 7pm. Family-appropriate before peak nightlife hours.

Name: TOPO Chiado
Address: Largo do Chiado Rua do Carmo 57 1200-092 Lisboa
Tips: Rooftop bar directly above Chiado with sweeping castle and rooftop views. Good food menu. Book a table for sunset.

Name: A Baiuca
Tips: The most raw and authentic fado experience in Alfama. Fado vadio where locals spontaneously stand up and sing when moved to — no set show no programme. Not a performance: mid-dinner someone at a nearby table rises and begins. Tiny impeccably local. Thu-Sat only. Book weeks ahead.

Name: Cantinho do Aziz
Tips: One of the most distinctive restaurants in Lisbon — Mozambican-Portuguese fusion reflecting Portugals colonial ties to East Africa. Run by the Aziz family since 1992. Matapa (cassava leaves with prawns and peanut sauce) is described as life-changing. Muamba chicken curry and piri-piri crab. An experience impossible to find elsewhere in Portugal. Book ahead.
